data.title: Balancing Near-Term Guidance Cuts With Long-Term Growth Drivers: Upholding a Buy on Boston Scientific
data.description:
In a report released today, Matthew Taylor from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on Boston Scientific, with a price target of $100.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Matthew Taylor has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that balance near-term headwinds with a favorable long-term setup. He acknowledges that Boston Scientific trimmed its 2026 outlook on slower growth in urology, electrophysiology, and WATCHMAN, and that guidance for both revenue and EPS has been reduced, prompting lower estimates and a cut to his price target.

At the same time, he argues the shares remain undervalued given the company’s exposure to faster-growing cardiovascular and structural heart markets and a robust innovation pipeline across multiple high-opportunity therapies. Taylor also expects recent and pending acquisitions, coupled with a sizeable share repurchase program, to support revenue acceleration beyond 2026 and drive further multiple expansion, underpinning his continued Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $100.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Alphabet: Strengthening AI and Cloud Infrastructure Moat Supports Durable Growth and Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ralph Schackart has maintained their bullish stance on GOOG stock, giving a Buy rating on April 20.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ralph Schackart has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Alphabet’s strengthening position in cloud and AI. He views the product announcements from Google Cloud Next—such as the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, next‑generation TPUs, and expanded cybersecurity tools—as evidence that Alphabet is evolving into a critical infrastructure provider for enterprise AI and data workloads.

Schackart believes these initiatives should deepen customer adoption, enhance monetization opportunities across Google Cloud, and reinforce Alphabet’s long‑term competitive moat in AI. In his assessment, the breadth of new “agentic” capabilities positions Alphabet to capture incremental share in a rapidly growing market, supporting durable revenue growth and justifying a positive outlook on the stock.

Schackart covers the Communication Services sector, focusing on stocks such as Meta Platforms, Alphabet Class C, and AppLovin. According to TipRanks, Schackart has an average return of 10.8% and a 55.88% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 20, J.P. Morgan also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $395.00 price target.

data.title: Immunic Buy Rating: Vidofludimus Calcium’s Differentiated Efficacy and Safety Profile vs. BTK Inhibitors in Relapsing MS
data.description:
William Blair analyst Myles Minter has reiterated their bullish stance on IMUX stock, giving a Buy rating on April 17.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Myles Minter has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors centered on Immunic’s lead asset, vidofludimus calcium, and its positioning versus emerging BTK inhibitors in relapsing multiple sclerosis. Recent fenebrutinib data from Roche underscore that brain-penetrant BTK inhibition can drive strong efficacy, but the accompanying liver and infection safety liabilities, including an imbalance in deaths and a potential need for boxed warnings, may limit broad use in the RMS population.

Against this backdrop, Minter highlights vidofludimus calcium’s differentiated mechanism as a next-generation DHODH inhibitor with markedly stronger potency than teriflunomide and added neuroprotective activity through Nurr1 activation, which could narrow or eliminate the efficacy gap with BTK agents. He also points to its cleaner safety profile to date, especially on liver parameters and absence of reported fatal events, as a key advantage heading into the pivotal ENSURE-1 and ENSURE-2 readouts expected by year-end 2026, supporting a favorable risk/reward and justifying a Buy rating on Immunic shares.

According to TipRanks, Minter is a top 100 analyst with an average return of 37.5% and a 60.81% success rate. Minter covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Dianthus Therapeutics, Immunic, and Xenon.

In another report released on April 17, Stifel Nicolaus also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a $2.50 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Driven Brands: Lowered Profit Outlook and Heightened Execution Risk Support Neutral Hold Rating and Reduced $14.25 Price Target
data.description:
Driven Brands Holdings, the Consumer Cyclical sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Mark Jordan, CFA from Goldman Sachs assigned a Hold rating on the stock and has a $14.25 price target.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mark Jordan, CFA has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors linked to Driven Brands’ updated financial outlook and rising uncertainty. He incorporates the company’s preliminary, unaudited results for 4Q25 and 1Q26 into his model and cuts the price target to $14.25, reflecting weaker visibility on near-term operating performance and overall business momentum.

He further trims his adjusted EBITDA forecasts for FY26, FY27, and FY28 to account for a softer first quarter in both sales and margins and more cautious expectations for the remainder of the forecast period. While the business still generates meaningful earnings, the reduced profit trajectory and heightened execution risk, in his view, do not yet justify a more constructive stance, leading him to maintain a neutral Hold recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
